world.title: ‘No unilateral decisions’ on Malacca Strait, ASEAN works by consensus: Malaysia's foreign minister
world.description: Malaysian Foreign Minister Mohamad Hasan’s comments came amid geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, with a chokepoint at the Strait of Hormuz, and after Indonesia Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa floated the idea of imposing a levy on ships passing through the Malacca Strait.

world.title: US-Iran War Live: Pakistan, US discuss Iran talks in Islamabad amid ceasefire push
world.description: US-Iran War Live Updates: Senior Iranian officials have accused Washington of stalling peace negotiations, citing a US naval blockade of Iranian ports as a key obstacle, while Iran’s Revolutionary Guard said it seized two foreign vessels in the Strait of Hormuz and fired on a third for alleged maritime violations. President Masoud Pezeshkian said Tehran remains open to “dialogue and agreement” but that breaches of commitments, blockades and threats are hindering meaningful talks. US President Donald Trump has signalled he remains prepared to resume military action if negotiations fail. Meanwhile, Lebanon condemned Israel over the killing of journalist Amal Khalil and the wounding of her colleague Zeinab Faraj, accusing it of violating a ceasefire through continued strikes and demolitions. Catch all the live updates at indiatoday.in.

world.title: $32.4M USDA Grant Funding Open for Local Agriculture Markets
world.description: The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) has announced $32.4 million in grant funding through the Local Agriculture Market Program (LAMP) to support the development and expansion of local and regional food systems across the United States. The funding aims to strengthen producer-to-consumer marketing, improve local food supply chains, and support the growth of food enterprises

business.title: Dubai home prices record first decline after post
business.description: Dubai's red-hot housing market is cooling for the first time since 2020, with prices and sales volume dropping in March. Regional conflict is impacting demand, though developers are offering incentives. Despite jitters, some see end-users driving continued, albeit slower, sales, with a focus on long-term resilience.
